rationale.R1.2: The artifact's paragraph order is broken: experimental results (paragraph 3) appear before the method and contribution are defined (paragraph 4), and paragraph 4 itself contains a forward-reference to 'the preceding paragraph' that is currently self-contradicting. Restoring a coherent premise→prior-work→contribution→evidence chain is an intrinsic and central demand of the restructuring task.
rationale.R1.5: The delegation is directly and exclusively a section-ordering request. The canonical IMRaD-style arc (setup → literature → contribution → experiments) is inverted in the artifact; correcting this section sequence is the load-bearing output. Failure to produce a sound narrative arc invalidates the delegation entirely.
